description: The ButtonValueShift component can be used to make an IButton add the ``delta`` amount to a value whenever it is pressed.

fields.Delta: By how much ``TargetValue`` should be shifted on each press.
fields.Min: The minimum value that can be shifted to.
fields.Max: The maximum value that can be shifted to.
fields.WrapAround: Whether or not the value should wrap around to the other extreme when reaching either ``Min`` or ``Max``.
fields.MaxIsExclusive: When the current amount is at the value before max, it will stop there, unless wrap around is enabled, which then wraps around from the max value - 1.

usage:
To function, the component simply needs to be attached to a slot that also has a button component attached to it.
From then on, pressing that button will activate the ButtonValueShift, making it shift its ``TargetValue`` by its ``Delta``.

notes.title: Related Issues
notes.text: It is known that you can go over the maximum by setting the ``delta`` higher than the max or min values, allowing the output to be outside the ranges provided.
